the four. Why? To answer, let me refer to a biblical passage, 2 Sam. 13:4: "And Amnon said unto him, I love Tamar, my brother Absalom's sister." Why not just say "my sister"? Because she was not a full sister--Amnon was the son of King David and "Ahinoam the Jezreelitess" (2 Sam. 3:2), while Tamar and Absalom were the children of King David and "Maacah the daughter of Talmai king of Geshur" (2…

CHAPTER 8. How Absalom Murdered Amnon, Who Had Forced His Own Sister;
And How He Was Banished And Afterwards Recalled By David.

There are several kinds of hatred. There is one who hates his companion because he harmed him in money matters or struck him, or shamed him, or slandered him. For all of these and similar causes, a person should not hate his companion and be silent about his grievance as it is said in connection with the wicked: "And Absalom did not speak with Amnon either bad or good for Absalom hated Amnon" (II…

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Rashi on I Kings 3:7:1
matches.x: A small lad. He was twelve years old. And this is how [his age] is calculated: “And he called his name Yedidyah.”10II Shmuel 12:25. At the same time Amnon raped Tamar, as it is stated, “And it was after this, and Avsholom [the son of Dovid] had a sister.”11Ibid., 13:1. At the end of, “two years, and Avsholom had sheep shearers,”12Ibid. v. 23. and he killed Amnon. Avsholom fled and went to Geshur,…
